rulings.Case Title: SANTIAGO ROBLETO VS. JULIA VARGAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further & Proper Responses To Vargas'S First Set Of Req.'S For Admissions; Memo Of P & A
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 6, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 1, DEFENDANT JULIA VARGAS' Re-Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further & Proper Responses To Vargas's First Set Of Request's For Admissions Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DENIED AS UNTIMELY NOTICED. CCP SEC.2033.290(C) REQUIRES THAT A NOTICE OF MOTION TO COMPEL MUST BE FILED WITHIN 45 DAYS AFTER RECEIPT OF THE ALLEGEDLY IMPROPER RESPONSE. IT APPEARS FROM THE PAPERS THAT DEFENDANT JULIA VARGAS DID FILE AN INITIAL NOTICE OF HER INTENT TO FILE A MOTION TO COMPEL WITHIN THE 45 DAYS SPECIFIED IN THE STATUTE; HOWEVER, WHEN IT WAS POINTED OUT BY PLAINTIFFS THAT THE PAPERS FILED WITH INITIAL MOTION DID NOT INCLUDE THE REQUIRED SEPARATE STATEMENT, DEFENDANT, RATHER THAN PROCEEDING WITH THE PROPERLY NOTICED MOTION, CHOSE TO WITHDRAW THE PENDING MOTION AND FILED WHAT IT DESCRIBED AS A"RE-NOTICE OF MOTION" ON OCTOBER 2, 2012. THIS NOTICE OF MOTION WAS OUTSIDE THE PERIOD PROVIDED FOR BY CCP SEC. 2033.290(C) AND A PARTY'S UNILATERAL ACTION IN EXTENDING THE PERIOD OF TIME IS INEFFECTIVE. THE COURT WILL IMPOSE NO MONETARY SANCTIONS AS PLAINTIFFS' RESPONSES TO THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION WERE CLEARLY IMPROPER AND, HAD THIS MOTION BEEN TIMELY FILED THE COURT WOULD HAVE ORDERED TO PLAINTIFFS TO EITHER ADMIT OR DENY THE REQUESTS. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER FOR SUBMISSION TO OPPOSING COUNSEL FOR AGREEMENT AS TO FORM AND PRESENT THE ORDER TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON NOVEMBER 6TH. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: IN RE APPLICATION OF:PURQI. COM, VS. MAHENDRA TRIVEDI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing Re; Petition For Relief Pursuant To Interstate And International Depositions And Discovery Act And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 6, 2012, Line 8, APPLICANT PURQUI. COMP Ntc Of Hearing Re; Petition For Relief Pursuant To Interstate And International Depositions And Discovery Act And Pos The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Shapiro as follows: Granted. CCP § 2029.600 allows for a party to challenge a subpoena issued by an out of state court when that subpoena is served on a witness in California. PurQi.com is a California entity which does not have any agent for service of process in Arizona, nor is it licensed to business in that state. Therefore, service of the subpoena, even though it was issued by a court of another jurisdiction, must comply with the Code of Civil Procedure. Trivedi failed to comply with the requirements of the subpoena and there is no evidence that plaintiff notified PurQi.com that it had been withdrawn prior to the filing of the motion to quash. PurQi.com is the prevailing party and is entitled to attorney's fees in the amount of $6,900.00. The court adds a payment date of December 6, 2012. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/EG

rulings.Case Title: TINA CHEN et al VS. RECOLOGY INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 6, 2012, Line 12, DEFENDANT RECOLOGY INC.,, GOLDEN GATE DISPOSAL & RECYCLING COMPANY, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT The Court denies summary judgment. The Court denies and grants summary adjudication in part. The Court denies summary adjudication as to the first cause of action for negligence because there is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant breached its duty of care. The Court denies summary adjudication as to the trespass cause of action because there is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether plaintiff had a possessory interest in the tree. The Court grants summary adjudication as to the nuisance cause of action. Plaintiff's principal claim is based on the view and any problems regarding noise were incidental. See Oliver v. AT&T Wireless (1999) 76 Cal.App.4th 521. The Court grants summary adjudication as to the NIED cause of action because it is not a distinct cause of action, see Potter v. Firestone Tire & Rubber Co., (1993) 6 Cal.4th 965, 984, and emotional distress damages are not recoverable for pure property damage. See Cooper v. Superior Court (1984) 153 Cal.App.3d 1008, 1012. The Court grants as to the IIED cause of action because there is no evidence of outrageous conduct. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/EG [PB]

rulings.Case Title: EDDIE VALADEZ VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Andmotion To Quash The Deposition Subpoena To Person Most Knowledgeable
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 6, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 2, PLAINTIFF EDDIE VALADEZ' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash The Deposition Subpoena To Person Most Knowledgeable Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DENIED. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO HAS THE RIGHT TO TAKE DISCOVERY ON THE ISSUE OF WHETHER OR NOT VALID SERVICE WAS MADE UPON ITS INDIVIDUAL EMPLOYEES AND THE FACT THAT A MOTION FILED ON BEHALF OF THE INDIVIDUAL EMPLOYEES IS PENDING DOES NOT MOOT OUT THE NEED FOR THIS DEPOSITION. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER FOR SUBMISSION TO OPPOSING COUNSEL FOR AGREEMENT AS TO FORM AND PRESENT THE ORDER TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON NOVEMBER 6TH. rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Stay Proceeding Pending Arbitration Pursuant To Pltfs' Demand For Arbitration
